item.title: Bill Gates appearing before House committee investigating Epstein
item.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates is sitting for a transcribed interview as revelations about his relationship with Epstein have rocked his philanthropic foundation.

item.title: U.S. will hit Iran "hard" again after "playing us for suckers," Trump says
item.description: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire appears to crumble as Trump says he's going to launch new strikes and Tehran will "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.

item.title: Michigan court overturns man's conviction in plot to kidnap Gov. Whitmer
item.description: In a 3-0 ruling, the Michigan Court of Appeals on Tuesday overturned a conviction against a man in connection with the 2020 plot to kidnap Gov. Gretchen Whitmer.

item.title: Trump signs $70 billion immigration bill, capping lengthy fight over ICE funding
item.description: President Trump signed a bill funding immigration enforcement agencies through the end of his term, bringing an end to a monthslong feud that exposed deep divisions on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Bill Gates speaks out before Jeffrey Epstein testimony
item.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates spoke to reporters on Capitol Hill Wednesday before testifying for members of the House Oversight Committee about his ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Epstein files triggered "freakout" in White House, new report shows
item.description: The release of the Epstein files triggered a "freakout" inside the White House for President Trump's top advisers, a new report from The New York Times shows. CBS News' Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: Nevada GOP Gov. Lombardo projected to face Democrat Ford in highly competitive race
item.description: Democrats are aiming to unseat Nevada GOP Gov. Joe Lombardo in one of the country's most tightly contested gubernatorial contests this year.

item.title: Trump asks Congress for short-term FISA extension amid impasse over Pulte role
item.description: President Trump said he's asking Congress to approve a short-term extension of a key spy authority to "provide time for the selection and confirmation of a permanent" director of national intelligence.

item.title: Oversight chair working to have Blanche testify in Epstein probe
item.description: House Oversight and Reform Committee Chairman James Comer said he wants Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to appear before lawmakers in July.

item.title: Why some experts are concerned about potential health risks during World Cup
item.description: Health officials are warning about the increased risk for potential disease outbreaks during the 2026 FIFA World Cup due to the lack of federal public health infrastructure and planning. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder shares more about how fans can protect themselves during mass gathering events.

item.title: MSG and NYPD to maintain heavy security for Game 4 of NBA Finals, Trump not attending
item.description: The NYPD and Madison Square Garden are maintaining heightened security for Game 4 of the NBA Finals between the Knicks and Spurs, despite President Trump not attending. CBS News' Jared Orchacher has more.

item.title: Bill Gates on Capitol Hill to testify on Jeffrey Epstein probe
item.description: Bill Gates is facing questions on Wednesday before members of the House Oversight Committee as part of its Jeffrey Epstein probe. CBS News' Taurean Small has more.

item.title: G Flip says they're "just so thankful" for song in "Off Campus," gives advice for young artists
item.description: Musician G Flip first rose to fame in Australia but has become a global star since their song "Bed of Fire" appeared in the series "Off Campus." They speak to "CBS Mornings" about how the song's popularity has impacted their music, family support and advice for young artists.

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade new round of strikes in wake of Apache helicopter attack
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday on social media that Iran has "taken too long to negotiate a deal" and will "pay the price," as Iran said it launched new attacks on U.S. installations across the Middle East. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Olivia Rinaldi have more.

item.title: Inflation topped 4% in May as CPI surged to highest level in 3 years
item.description: The Consumer Price Index rose last month at a 4.2% annual rate amid a spike in U.S. energy prices.

item.title: Americans face accelerating inflation pressures amid Iran war, growing energy prices
item.description: Household budgets are under increased pressure as new Consumer Price Index data shows inflation rose to 4.2%, the highest level since April 2023. The Federal Reserve will meet later in June to discuss interest rates. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady breaks it down.

item.title: How much of your Social Security can a student loan default take?
item.description: Your Social Security check isn't fully shielded from old federal student loans. Here's how much is at risk.

item.title: Graham Platner will face Susan Collins in high-stakes Maine Senate race, CBS News projects
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner will face Republican incumbent Sen. Susan Collins in the 2026 midterm election, CBS News projects after Tuesday's primary. Caitlin Huey-Burns has more.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace fails to advance in GOP primary for South Carolina governor
item.description: Pamela Evette and Alan Wilson are heading to a runoff on June 23 in South Carolina's Republican primary for governor.

item.title: Amazon's best books of the year so far
item.description: Amazon Books editorial director Sarah Gelman joins "CBS Mornings" to reveal Amazon's best books of the year so far and why they made the list.

item.title: Pilot allegedly flew 900 flights without valid license: "Like a movie script"
item.description: "This is similar to a doctor that is licensed to practice family medicine but is doing brain surgery in their office," police said.

item.title: Mirra Andreeva rises to fame as Serena Williams returns to tennis
item.description: Mirra Andreeva joins CBS News' Errol Barnett to discuss her rise to fame and her next challenge -- Wimbledon. This comes as Serena Williams returns to the court.

item.title: Alabama nitrogen gas executions are cruel and unusual punishment, judge says
item.description: A federal judge banned Alabama from executing an inmate by nitrogen hypoxia, calling the method unconstitutionally cruel.

item.title: Working remotely can hurt people's mental health, research finds
item.description: Although working remotely offers flexibility, it also takes a toll on people's mental health, experts said.

item.title: Karmelo Anthony found guilty of murder in stabbing of Austin Metcalf
item.description: The trial of Karmelo Anthony centered on whether he acted in self-defense when he fatally stabbed Austin Metcalf at a Frisco ISD track meet in 2025.

item.title: Violent anti-immigration protests erupt in Belfast after stabbing attack
item.description: Despite calls for calm, masked anti-immigration protesters in Belfast set a bus, vehicles and some homes on fire in what one politician called "a race-based pogrom."

item.title: Passersby left shocked after burning cross spotted in Grant Park
item.description: The flames from the cross burning in the middle of Grant Park were visible to anyone driving or walking down Columbus Drive.

item.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
item.description: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

item.title: DOJ finds EEOC violated law by pressuring employers to make race-based decisions
item.description: The Justice Department accused the EEOC of violating civil rights laws by issuing guidelines that effectively pressured employers to make race-based considerations in hiring and promotions.
